result.diagram_code:
sequenceDiagram
participant Model
participant ProVerif
Model->>ProVerif: Load NeedhamSchroederPK_bug_02.pv
Note over Model,ProVerif: Parsing fails before any protocol run is analyzed
ProVerif-->>Model: Syntax error at line 138, characters 15-17
result.explanation: This case does not show a protocol attack trace. ProVerif stops at parsing with a syntax error, so the Needham-Schroeder PK model is never analyzed. The file visibly contains a corrupted inserted fragment near the process-launch section for the server (the comment beginning "Launch an unboulet ..."), which is consistent with accidental text corruption in the .pv file rather than a cryptographic flaw demonstrated by the log.
result.patch_suggestion: Apply a purely local syntax cleanup: remove the stray injected text near the server-launch comment and restore that block to a normal comment, e.g. replace the corrupted two-line comment before `(!processS(skS)) |` with `(* Launch an unbounded number of sessions of the server *)`. This is the minimal fix supported by the evidence because ProVerif currently fails before verification begins.
result.notes: The provided evidence only confirms a parse failure, not a secrecy or authentication violation. If parsing still fails after restoring the corrupted comment block, re-check the nearby initiator code around line 138, since ProVerif's reported location may be slightly downstream from the actual malformed text.
